name: nav_menu_uppercase_convention
description: Where the app renders navigation, menu, and button labels (HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, STORE, CLIPS, PHOTOS, VIDEO, LIVE), they keep the uppercase convention consistently.

name: action_labels_specific
description: Where the app renders primary action labels, they use the mandated specific labels (STORE, Subscribe) rather than generic Submit/OK alone when a specific label is specified.

name: newsletter_errors_name_email_and_fix
description: Where the app renders newsletter error messages, they name the email field and state the fix (valid address format) rather than only saying Invalid.

name: exact_mandated_chrome_strings
description: Where the app renders mandated chrome copy, the exact strings appear: tab title 2025 Apex Grand Prix Driver — Avery Vale; preloader LOAD VALE; menu items HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR; socials CLIPS, PHOTOS, VIDEO, LIVE; contact hello@averyvale.example; hero widget NEXT RACE, ALPINE GP, and NOVA RACING SINCE 2019; legal links Privacy Policy, Terms, Cookies; press-kit tabs JSON, Markdown, ICS; nav PRESS KIT; race circuits Alpine GP, Bayfront Circuit, Ridgeway GP, Solstice GP, Meridian Night Run, Cascade Finale.

name: body_copy_spelling_grammar
description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy on the Avery Vale homepage, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.

name: avery_vale_terminology_consistent
description: Where the app renders the driver and team identity, terminology stays consistent as Avery Vale / AVERY VALE and Nova Racing (not mixed with leftover source-driver naming).

name: supporting_copy_sentence_case
description: Where the app renders supporting body copy (not nav/menu/button labels), it uses one consistent sentence-case convention.

name: newsletter_confirmation_states_success
description: Where the app renders the newsletter confirmation, it states that the signup succeeded rather than a vague affirmation alone.

name: press_kit_empty_state_plain_language
description: Where the app renders press-kit empty states, the copy says selection lists are empty in plain language rather than a blank or cryptic error code.

name: status_and_import_errors_name_fields
description: Race status validation errors name the status field, and press-kit Import errors name the import problem in plain language rather than silent failure or generic Error text.

name: dual_tone_scarcity_palette
description: The composition reads as a dual-tone cinematic elite-motorsport profile: sections compute to the slate-black dark-green base rgb(40, 44, 32) with off-white rgb(244, 244, 237) text, the hero is a lighter cream band, and neon lime rgb(210, 255, 0) is the single loud accent reserved for the Store button, current-menu stroke, and hover/highlight states rather than flooding large section backgrounds

name: wordmark_serif_sans_pairing
description: The stacked AVERY VALE wordmark pairs a serif display treatment for AVERY with a heavy Mona Sans treatment for VALE, both about 32px at 1440, and oversized display copy (impact statement, menu items, footer statement) renders in the bundled open-license serif display face

name: menu_overlay_composition
description: The open menu is a full-screen dark-green overlay with a topographic line texture, oversized uppercase display-face menu items (roughly 96px at 1440) on the left, a staggered four-image fictional editorial grid on the right, and the current HOME item de-emphasized to the muted green rgb(180, 184, 165) with a lime stroke

name: helmet_mask_and_marquee_fade
description: Helmet cards are tall portrait cards (about 445 by 594 at 1440, three across) masked to a helmet silhouette and the logo marquees fade out at their left and right edges

name: impact_statement_metrics
description: The impact heading renders in the bundled serif display face at roughly 132px with a line-height near 109px in the lime-off color rgb(178, 199, 58) at 1440x900

name: hero_cream_band_contrast
description: The hero band background computes to rgb(239, 239, 229) and contrasts against the following sections whose background computes to rgb(40, 44, 32)

name: wordmark_weights_and_color
description: The wordmark renders AVERY in a bundled serif family at weight 400 and VALE in Mona Sans Variable at weight 900, both in the dark color rgb(17, 17, 18)

name: store_button_pill_treatment
description: The Store button is a filled lime pill with a small bag icon, a rounded radius near 12px, and a bold variable-sans label near 20px, and the hamburger and menu close control are square outlined buttons about 60px by 60px with rounded corners

name: footer_statement_metrics
description: The footer statement renders in the bundled serif display face at roughly 72px in the off-white rgb(244, 244, 237), with its characters filling into the lime-off color

name: newsletter_footer_treatment
description: The newsletter email field is a dark input with off-white text and a visible focus ring, the Subscribe control uses the lime accent with dark text, and the inline error renders in a clearly distinct warning treatment

name: desktop_nav_single_row
description: At viewport widths at or above 992px the nav is a single row about 100px tall with the wordmark left, the monogram mark centered, and STORE, PRESS KIT, and hamburger on the right

name: mobile_390_adaptation
description: At 390px width the nav wraps to about 140px tall with the AV monogram and a centered AVERY VALE wordmark (about 24px) plus the NOVA RACING SINCE 2019 sub-line stacked, the helmet grid collapses to a single column with cards about 326 by 435, the impact heading drops to about 48px, and the menu photo grid is hidden

name: fluid_type_across_breakpoints
description: Resizing across the 992, 991, 767, and 479 pixel breakpoints scales type fluidly via clamp() or equivalent continuous viewport sizing rather than jumping abruptly between adjacent widths

name: case_conventions_consistent
description: Navigation, menu, and button labels keep the uppercase convention while supporting body copy uses one consistent sentence-case convention

name: asymmetric_hero_baseline_grid
description: The hero composes asymmetrically rather than as an equal-column stack: the driver silhouette anchors the center field, the NEXT RACE widget sits lower-left, and the fixed nav occupies the top edge, with section spacing and card gaps aligning to a consistent 1.25rem (20px) baseline unit and its multiples

name: clamp_fluid_display_type
description: Display type for the impact statement, menu item labels, footer brand statement, and preloader word uses fluid clamp() (or equivalent continuous viewport scaling) so sizes change smoothly across widths rather than jumping only at breakpoints

name: three_tier_token_surfaces
description: Computed styles resolve the three-tier brand tokens: surface colors dark-green #282c20 / cream #efefe5 / off-white #f4f4ed, text colors black #111112 / off-white #f4f4ed / muted greens #dde1d2 and #b4b8a5, and accent colors lime #d2ff00 / lime-off #b2c73a / orange #ff6b00

name: original_media_density_and_variety
description: The newly authored media matches the reference's visual density without copying it: the horizontal strip has six distinct editorial scenes, the menu uses a separate staggered set of at least four photos, and the social poster/video form a purposefully art-directed racing composition rather than gradients, repeated stock thumbnails, or generic placeholders

name: layered_hero_art_direction
description: At 1440x900 the fictional racer portrait/cutout, contour field, translucent blobs, soft shadow or reflection planes, and lower-left race widget overlap as an intentional depth composition; the hero is not a single rectangular image or empty cream field

name: fictional_helmet_and_partner_craft
description: The three fictional helmet cards have recognizably different surface systems with shaped masks, highlights, shadows, and distinct reveal states, while the five fictional partner marks vary in symbol/wordmark construction but share a controlled monochrome optical weight

name: physical_3d_and_crisp_vector_assets
description: The hero's original debranded 3D object shows visibly different matte, glossy, and reflective material responses under environment lighting, and the original interactive vector motif remains crisp at desktop and mobile sizes rather than appearing as a blurry raster substitute

name: newsletter_copy_actionable
description: The newsletter inline error copy names the email field and states how to fix the input, and the post-submit confirmation copy states that the signup succeeded rather than a vague affirmation

name: race_calendar_list_anatomy
description: Race calendar rows share one dense list anatomy (circuit, date, status chip, selected lime treatment) on the dark-green base rgb(40, 44, 32).

name: press_kit_and_palette_visual_system
description: The press-kit drawer uses the dark-green surface and off-white body type with lime tab/active states for JSON / Markdown / ICS; the command palette is a centered dark overlay with a single search field and compact results list.

name: menu_open_shows_home_stroke
description: Clicking the hamburger opens a full-viewport dark-green menu overlay; HOME shows the lime telemetry-stroke current-item line and ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR do not.

name: menu_item_scrolls_and_closes
description: With the menu open, activating ON TRACK closes the overlay and scrolls within the same homepage (/) to the horizontal media strip without loading another document or route.

name: menu_x_closes_preserving_scroll
description: With the menu open, activating the X control closes the overlay while leaving page scroll position unchanged; the fixed navigation bar stays pinned and the homepage is interactive again.

name: newsletter_invalid_keeps_subscribe_disabled
description: Typing an invalid email (missing @ or domain without a dot) into the footer newsletter field leaves Subscribe disabled and shows an inline error naming the email field; correcting to fan@averyvale.example clears the error and enables Subscribe.

name: newsletter_valid_submit_confirms_and_clears
description: Submitting the newsletter form with fan@averyvale.example shows an inline confirmation in the footer, empties the field, sets press-kit JSON newsletter to that email, and never reloads or navigates away.

name: video_hover_play_leave_pause_cycle
description: Hovering the social video card starts muted looping playback and fades the placeholder away; moving the pointer off pauses playback and restores the placeholder to fully opaque; a second hover-and-leave cycle still works with no stuck intermediate state.

name: reload_baseline_resets_homepage_state
description: After opening the menu, selecting a race, shortlisting a helmet, typing into the newsletter field, and hovering the video card, reloading returns the initial state: LOAD VALE preloader plays again, menu/press kit/palette closed, selections and shortlist empty, newsletter empty with no confirmation, and no prior visit state persists.

name: menu_destinations_stay_on_homepage
description: Activating HOME, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR from the open menu each closes the overlay and scrolls within / to the hero, social video section, and race calendar respectively — no second document or origin navigation occurs.

name: chrome_activations_stay_in_page
description: Activating the AVERY VALE wordmark, AV monogram, STORE button, social labels (CLIPS, PHOTOS, VIDEO, LIVE), business contact, or legal links either scrolls to a homepage region or performs a no-op — none reload the document or navigate to another origin.

name: post_menu_close_nav_still_pinned
description: After either menu close path (menu item activation or X), the fixed navigation bar remains pinned at the top and no document reload has occurred at any step.

name: press_kit_flow_export_reflects_session
description: Press kit flow: shortlist one helmet and one editorial card and select three distinct races so Selected races reads 3; open PRESS KIT; confirm JSON names those three circuits and both shortlisted labels, each races entry shows id/circuit/date/status/selected/uid, and each shortlist entry shows kind/label/index; switch to ICS and confirm exactly three VEVENT entries with UID matching race uids; switch to Markdown and confirm Avery Vale plus the same races and shortlist; activate Copy and see a copied confirmation.

name: undo_redo_shortlist_flow
description: Undo flow: shortlist one helmet so the shortlist count increases by one, activate Undo so the helmet leaves and the count returns, then activate Redo so the helmet and count restore.

name: command_palette_calendar_flow
description: Command palette flow: press Meta+K or Control+K, type CALENDAR, activate Go CALENDAR; the palette closes and the page scrolls within / to the race calendar without a document reload.

name: race_status_edit_flow
description: Race status edit flow: open Edit status on Ridgeway GP, set Upcoming, submit; chip reads Upcoming; when that race is selected the ICS STATUS line reads CONFIRMED; set Completed again and chip/ICS STATUS return to Completed/CANCELLED.

name: press_kit_export_import_round_trip
description: Export/import round trip: select two races and shortlist one helmet, note the JSON preview, Undo until Selected races is 0 and shortlist empty, Import that JSON; Selected races returns to 2, shortlist to 1, and previews again name those circuits and the helmet label.

name: console_clean_during_flows
description: No console errors or unhandled promise rejections appear while the homepage and its interaction workflows (preloader dismiss, menu open/close, race select, shortlist, press kit, command palette, undo/redo, hovers, scroll-linked sections, hover-to-play video, marquees, newsletter submit) are exercised

name: all_assets_same_origin
description: Loading the homepage issues zero network asset requests to a non-origin host; fonts, images, video, CSS, JS, WASM, and GL assets are all same-origin

name: fonts_resolve_bundled
description: Rendered text resolves to the bundled Mona Sans Variable sans face and the bundled open-license serif display face rather than a Times or system fallback

name: brand_tokens_exact
description: The brand tokens compute to their exact values — lime #d2ff00, dark-green #282c20, white #f4f4ed, black #111112, lime-off #b2c73a, green-off-white-1 #dde1d2, green-off-white-2 #b4b8a5, and orange #ff6b00 — and the body background computes to rgb(40, 44, 32)

name: storage_stays_empty
description: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after the homepage workflows are exercised

name: rive_wasm_same_origin
description: The required original interactive .riv asset and its Rive runtime WASM both load successfully from same-origin paths, contribute to a visible vector motif, and issue no request to a Rive or package CDN host

name: gl_assets_same_origin
description: The required original GLB, base-color/roughness/metallic texture maps, local HDR environment, and any Draco/KTX2 decoder files load successfully from same-origin paths and contribute to the visible WebGL hero

name: store_button_computed_colors
description: The Store button background computes to rgb(210, 255, 0) with text rgb(17, 17, 18), and the menu overlay and footer backgrounds compute to rgb(40, 44, 32)

name: nav_dimensions_desktop
description: The nav bar row is about 100px tall at 1440x900 and the hamburger button is about 60px by 60px

name: menu_modal_focus_behavior
description: The open menu overlay behaves as a modal dialog: it closes when Escape is pressed, traps focus while open, and returns focus to the hamburger button on close

name: keyboard_operable_with_focus_ring
description: Every interactive control (Store button, PRESS KIT, hamburger, menu items, socials, legal links, shortlist controls, race rows, Undo, Redo, the email field, Subscribe) is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone and shows a visible focus indicator

name: icon_buttons_accessible_names
description: The icon-only hamburger and menu close controls expose descriptive accessible names

name: split_text_accessible_phrase
description: Split-text headlines keep the original phrase as an aria-label on the heading container while the individual character spans are hidden from the accessibility tree

name: newsletter_aria_live_announcements
description: The newsletter inline error and confirmation are announced through an aria-live region as well as shown visually

name: video_stays_muted
description: The social video is muted and never plays audio at any point while the card is exercised

name: interactive_fast_no_layout_shift
description: The homepage is interactive within about 2 seconds of a local cold load, and after first paint no visible layout jumps occur as fonts, images, or the hero scene finish loading — media regions hold their space from the start

name: smooth_full_page_scroll
description: Continuous scrolling from top to bottom shows no visible hitching or dropped frames through every animated section

name: hydration_clean_console
description: The console is hydration-clean: no hydration mismatch errors or warnings appear on first load or after exercising the interactive islands (menu, Store, PRESS KIT, race calendar, press kit, palette, video card, newsletter form), and statically rendered content is not visibly replaced by different client content after hydration

name: wcag_aa_contrast_surfaces
description: Text over the cream hero and dark-green sections, plus control labels including the lime Store button with dark text, meet WCAG AA contrast

name: semantic_landmarks_and_hero_canvas
description: The page chrome uses semantic landmarks (header, nav, main, footer) and native buttons for interactive controls, and the hero canvas or WebGL layer is either aria-hidden when a static silhouette sibling conveys the artwork or carries role img with a descriptive aria-label

name: deep_link_homepage_only
description: Loading route / directly renders the same single homepage baseline and chrome as a fresh in-app load; no secondary routes are required or implemented for this task

name: required_authored_asset_files_load
description: Browser network evidence shows successful same-origin loads with no 404s or decode errors for the required authored asset families: editorial raster images and SVG marks, a VP9 WebM with changing frames, an original GLB with material textures and HDR environment, and an original Rive file with its runtime WASM

name: shared_state_coherence_press_kit
description: Shared-state coherence: selecting a race or shortlisting an asset updates the Selected races / shortlist count and the press-kit preview content immediately without reload; Undo restores both chrome counts and kit preview together.

name: api_shaped_schemas_drive_forms
description: Newsletter Subscribe, race status edit, and press-kit Import validate through client-side schemas: invalid email/status/import JSON shows named field/import errors before commit; a valid Subscribe or status edit commits a record matching the declared field contract into the same store the press-kit JSON/ICS previews read.

name: desktop_nav_single_row_above_992
description: At desktop widths at or above 992px the nav is a single row about 100px tall with the AVERY VALE wordmark left, the AV monogram centered, and STORE, PRESS KIT, and hamburger right.

name: mobile_tap_targets_adequate
description: At 390px, primary interactive controls (hamburger, menu close, STORE, Subscribe) present tap targets at least about 44px in height or width.

name: fluid_type_across_breakpoints
description: Between 1440 and 390 widths, display and body type use fluid clamp()/vw scaling so sizes change continuously rather than jumping between adjacent widths at the 992, 991, 767, and 479 breakpoints.

name: no_clip_or_overflow_at_key_widths
description: At 1440 and 390 widths, content never clips or overflows the viewport.

name: mobile_nav_wraps_at_390
description: At 390px the nav wraps to about 140px tall, the AV monogram and a centered AVERY VALE wordmark (about 24px) with the NOVA RACING SINCE 2019 sub-line become visible and stacked, and the menu photo grid is hidden.

name: helmet_grid_single_column_at_390
description: At 390px the helmet grid collapses to a single column (card about 326 by 435) and the impact heading drops to about 48px.

name: mobile_hamburger_opens_menu
description: At 390px, tapping the hamburger opens the full-screen menu overlay and the overlay remains operable.

name: no_horizontal_scrollbar_at_390
description: At 390px width no horizontal scrollbar appears and content does not force horizontal page scroll.

name: media_and_hero_resize
description: Hero artwork, horizontal media cards, helmet cards, and the social video card size responsively to fit the layout at 1440 and 390 without fixed-size overflow.

name: fixed_nav_accessible_all_widths
description: The fixed navigation remains reachable and operable at 1440 and 390 after scrolling through sections.

name: showcase_composition_reflow_at_390
description: At 390 the asymmetric hero (silhouette, NEXT RACE widget, fixed nav) and helmet grid are re-choreographed into a stacked mobile composition that preserves hierarchy rather than a flat equal-column dump of the desktop grid.

name: calendar_and_press_kit_at_390
description: At 390px the race calendar rows stack date/status chips without horizontal overflow, the press-kit drawer uses the full viewport width, and the command palette search field and results stay inside the viewport.

name: cold_start_interactive_under_2s
description: The homepage is interactive within about 2 seconds of a local cold load.

name: console_clean_on_full_exercise
description: Loading and exercising the full page (preloader dismiss, menu open/close, race select, shortlist toggles, press kit open with format switches and copy, command palette open/filter/activate, hover states, scroll-linked sections, hover-to-play video, marquees, newsletter submit, undo/redo) emits no console errors, hydration warnings, or unhandled promise rejections.

name: chrome_interactions_stay_responsive
description: Menu open/close, STORE hover, and Subscribe respond without multi-second lag after first paint (chrome interactions feel under ~100ms to begin).

name: preloader_clears_without_hang
description: The LOAD VALE preloader clears within about one to two seconds and never permanently blocks interaction while heavier media finish streaming.

name: long_page_scroll_without_lag
description: Scrolling the full long-form homepage (hero through footer) remains usable without perceived multi-second freezes between sections.

name: interactive_during_media_stream
description: The UI stays interactive while heavier media, 3D, and vector-animation assets finish streaming in — nav, menu, and newsletter remain operable.

name: scroll_sections_hold_smooth_frame_rate
description: Continuous scrolling from top to bottom shows no visible hitching or dropped frames through every animated section (pinned horizontal media, impact fill, marquees, footer).

name: rapid_menu_clicks_never_freeze
description: Rapidly clicking the hamburger and X several times never freezes the page.

name: extended_scroll_session_stable
description: After an extended scroll-through with menu open/close, video hover cycles, and newsletter submit, the page remains responsive without runaway resource use that freezes interaction.

name: layout_stable_while_assets_load
description: After first paint no visible layout jumps occur as fonts, images, or the hero scene finish loading; media regions hold their space from the start.

name: scroll_linked_media_frame_rate
description: Scroll-linked horizontal media travel and section storytelling hold a smooth frame rate through the full page length on a 1440-wide viewport with no visible hitching during continuous scroll.

name: smooth_scroll_preserves_sticky_pin
description: In-page smooth-scroll jumps (HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, wordmark) feel continuous and settle naturally without lag, while the horizontal pin's position:sticky relationship stays intact.

name: layout_stable_no_reflow_jumps
description: Layout is stable after load: no visible reflow jumps as fonts, images, or the hero scene finish loading — media and embed regions reserve their space from first paint.

name: webgl_capability_fallback
description: WebGL/3D features detect capability before initializing and fall back to a complete static hero composition when GPU support is missing — the page remains whole, navigable, and visually coherent without the scene.

name: heavy_assets_load_progressively
description: Heavy assets load progressively: the page becomes interactive before 3D models, textures, and animation files finish loading; scene regions hold reserved space while streaming in; no interaction is blocked waiting on an asset.

name: press_kit_regen_stays_responsive
description: Opening the press kit and regenerating previews after selection/shortlist changes stays responsive without freezing the page.

name: preloader_animates_out
description: On a fresh real page load the lime LOAD VALE preloader animates out (fading and clearing) to reveal the page and does not stay covering content

name: menu_open_close_animates
description: Clicking the real hamburger animates the dark-green overlay open from a clipped shape to full-screen, and the X and Escape controls animate it closed rather than snapping

name: store_button_split_reveal
description: Hovering the STORE button runs a split-text reveal where the label slides up to expose an identical copy over about 0.4s

name: text_link_hover_color_shift
description: Hovering nav or footer text links (ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, the socials, the legal links) shifts their color toward lime or lime-off

name: signature_pinned_horizontal_track
description: Signature interaction on a fresh load: scrolling vertically through the pinned horizontal media section drives the media row sideways as a scroll-linked transform tied to scroll progress so later cards scroll into view, and scrolling back reverses the travel rather than leaving a static non-pinned row

name: helmet_card_hover_choreography
description: Hovering a helmet card with the real pointer scales its base image about 1.1x, wipes in a masked reveal image via a clip-path change, and turns the card index label lime

name: video_hover_play_and_fade
description: Hovering the social video card plays the video and fades its placeholder image from opaque to transparent, and leaving the card pauses playback and restores the placeholder

name: marquees_scroll_when_in_view
description: The collaborator and footer partner marquees translate continuously once their strip is in view

name: split_text_sequential_fill
description: On a fresh load, scrolling the impact statement and footer statement into view fills their characters into the highlight color in sequence, one after another rather than all at once

name: reduced_motion_respected
description: Under prefers-reduced-motion reduce the page settles immediately rather than hanging on scroll-linked animation, the preloader does not block content, in-page jumps use instant scroll, continuous marquees do not run, and press-kit/palette enter transitions collapse to immediate show/hide

name: menu_transition_timing_spec
description: On the real hamburger click the menu overlay transitions clip-path and visibility over 0.75s on cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1), with getAnimations on the real element reporting a 750ms duration with that easing

name: marquee_keyframes_paused_offscreen
description: The collaborator and footer marquees translate via the translateXLeft and translateXRight keyframes on a 30-second linear infinite loop and stay animation-play-state paused until their strip is in view

name: video_fade_timing_spec
description: The social video placeholder fades from opacity 1 to 0 via a 0.3s opacity transition on hover

name: helmet_reveal_timing_spec
description: On a real pointer hover, the helmet card reveal image transitions clip-path and transform over 0.75s on cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1)

name: newsletter_confirmation_animates
description: After submitting the newsletter form with a valid email, the inline confirmation message animates in with a short fade or slide of roughly 200 to 300 milliseconds rather than appearing instantly

name: inertial_easing_on_chrome
description: Menu overlay open/close, Store split reveal, and helmet reveal use the shared non-linear cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1) easing (getAnimations reports that easing) rather than mechanical linear tweens

name: smooth_scroll_keeps_sticky_pin
description: Activating an in-page menu destination (HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, or CALENDAR) eases the page to the target section with smooth scrolling while the horizontal media pin's sticky relationship stays intact and the scroll-linked track remains synchronized

name: scroll_story_section_sequence
description: On a fresh load, continuous vertical scrolling advances through the cream hero, the pinned horizontal media chapter, the impact statement character fill, the helmet grid, the race calendar, the collaborators marquee, the social video card, and the footer statement fill as a sequential narrative rather than unrelated static blocks

name: interactive_vector_motif_reacts
description: Using the real UI path on a fresh load, hovering, pressing, opening the menu, or advancing scroll progress causes the original local vector motif to transition crisply between states rather than remaining static or autoplaying an unrelated identical loop

name: press_kit_open_close_animates
description: Opening PRESS KIT via the real control expands the drawer/dialog with about a 0.3s opacity/transform transition on cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1); closing reverses it rather than snapping.

name: command_palette_enter_animates
description: Opening the command palette with Meta+K/Control+K enters with a short ~0.2s fade/scale on cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1); filtering updates results without layout jank.

name: race_and_shortlist_select_transition
description: Selecting a race or shortlisting an asset via the real UI shows an immediate ~0.2s selected-state transition (lime accent or check) rather than a hard snap with no feedback.

name: execution_quality_of_signature_interactions
description: Score execution quality of the reference experience (not invention beyond it): menu ellipse open/close, STORE split-text reveal, helmet clip-path reveal, and video hover-to-play feel polished and coherent rather than unfinished stubs.

name: scroll_storytelling_execution
description: On a fresh load, scroll-triggered storytelling (pinned horizontal media travel, impact/footer character fill, marquee in-view start) executes smoothly as a choreographed sequence rather than a janky or missing recreation of the reference motion.

name: preloader_and_hero_depth_execution
description: The LOAD VALE page-transition preloader and layered hero depth scene (portrait, contours, blobs, NEXT RACE widget) execute as a finished sequence matching the reference ambition.

name: avery_vale_brand_narrative_arc
description: The long-form page reads as one Avery Vale elite-motorsport narrative arc from cream hero through pinned media, impact statement, helmet study, race calendar, collaborators, social video, and footer — with the press kit as the useful end-state — rather than disconnected template blocks.

name: designed_experience_narrative_arc
description: The page reads as a designed experience with a narrative arc — sections build on each other visually and the signature pinned horizontal-media scroll interaction is memorable as faithful reference execution, not a template with effects sprinkled on.

name: showcase_rechoreographed_at_narrow_widths
description: Impeccable execution across breakpoints: at 390 the showcase composition is re-choreographed (wrapped nav, single-column helmet grid, hidden menu photo grid) rather than a broken shrink of the desktop layout.

name: press_kit_execution_quality
description: Score execution quality of the press-kit end state: live-compiled JSON/Markdown/ICS previews, copy confirmation, and selection-sensitive regeneration feel finished rather than stubbed static text.

name: palette_and_undo_execution
description: Command palette fuzzy navigation and undo/redo of shortlist/race mutations execute as polished operator tools rather than unfinished shortcuts.

name: innovation_catchall
description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable execution-quality enhancement of the reference experience that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick, and must not invent design beyond the reference. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.

name: rapid_hamburger_x_settles_open_or_closed
description: Rapidly clicking the hamburger and the X control in succession never leaves the menu overlay stuck half-open or the page inert; the menu always settles fully open or fully closed.

name: escape_while_menu_closed_noop
description: Pressing Escape while the menu is closed and no other overlay is open changes nothing on the page (overlay stays closed; scroll and chrome unchanged).

name: empty_newsletter_submit_blocked
description: Submitting the newsletter form with an empty email field is blocked: an inline message names the email field and no confirmation appears.

name: double_subscribe_one_confirmation
description: Double-activating the Subscribe control on one valid email shows exactly one confirmation message.

name: video_leave_mid_fade_restores_placeholder
description: Moving the pointer off the social video card mid-fade restores the placeholder image to fully opaque and pauses the video.

name: webgl_unavailable_static_hero_usable
description: If WebGL rendering is unavailable, the hero shows a static composition of the same artwork and every section, control, and flow (menu, newsletter, video hover, race calendar, shortlist, press kit, palette) remains present and usable.

name: newsletter_errors_name_email_field
description: Newsletter validation errors name the email field with actionable text rather than only saying Invalid.

name: newsletter_controls_semantic
description: The newsletter email field and Subscribe control use semantic input/button elements with visible labels (or accessible name equivalents) rather than unlabeled div shells.

name: menu_close_paths_work
description: The open menu overlay closes via the X control and via Escape, restoring an interactive homepage under the fixed nav.

name: subscribe_disabled_until_valid_email
description: The Subscribe control stays disabled until the newsletter field holds a contract-valid email (exactly one @, non-empty local part, domain segment containing a dot).

name: escape_closes_press_kit_or_palette
description: Pressing Escape while the press kit or command palette is open closes that overlay and returns focus to the control that opened it.

name: filter_preserves_hidden_selections
description: Filtering the race calendar to Completed then back to All keeps previously selected Upcoming races selected; the Selected races count does not drop solely because a filter hid rows.

name: empty_press_kit_valid_previews
description: Opening the press kit with zero selected races and zero shortlisted assets shows valid empty-state previews (JSON with schemaVersion 1, empty races/shortlist, newsletter none, ICS with zero VEVENT entries) rather than an error blank.

name: empty_undo_redo_noop
description: Undo when the undo stack is empty is a no-op that does not clear unrelated UI; Redo when the redo stack is empty is likewise a no-op.

name: out_of_enum_status_rejected
description: Submitting the race status editor with a value outside Upcoming or Completed is blocked with an inline error naming status and the race chip does not change.

name: malformed_import_keeps_state
description: Importing malformed or undecodable press-kit JSON, or JSON that violates the field contract (wrong schemaVersion, missing races, non-YYYY-MM-DD date, status outside Upcoming/Completed, shortlist kind outside helmet/editorial, empty or over-length shortlist label, shortlist index < 1, newsletter neither none nor a contract-valid email), shows a visible error naming the import problem, leaves selections and shortlist unchanged, and produces no console errors.

name: spacing_matches_20px_baseline
description: At 1440 width, section spacing and card gaps align to a consistent baseline unit of 1.25rem (20px) and its multiples rather than arbitrary one-off offsets visible in computed styles.

name: typography_matches_spec_metrics
description: At 1440 typography matches the spec: stacked AVERY (serif ~32px) / VALE (sans weight 900 ~32px) wordmark in rgb(17, 17, 18); impact statement roughly 132px / ~109px line-height in lime-off rgb(178, 199, 58); footer statement roughly 72px in off-white rgb(244, 244, 237); STORE label ~20px weight 800.

name: block_fidelity_fixed_nav_bar
description: Block: fixed nav bar — pinned top chrome with the stacked serif/heavy-sans AVERY VALE wordmark at left, centered monogram mark, filled lime STORE pill with bag icon, and square outlined hamburger at right, sitting over both cream and dark-green sections (reference: segment-01.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_hero_next_race_widget
description: Block: hero with NEXT RACE widget — full-viewport cream band with faint contour lines, large racer silhouette center-right, and a bordered lower-left NEXT RACE widget carrying an eyebrow, circuit squiggle, event label, laurel mark, and team line (reference: segment-01.png–segment-02.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_horizontal_media_strip
description: Block: pinned horizontal media strip — dark-green pinned section with a row of at least six tall rounded editorial cards (ON TRACK, SPEED, OFF TRACK, FOCUS, GARAGE, PODIUM) in duotone lime tint with lower-left labels, travelling sideways on vertical scroll (reference: segment-02.png–segment-06.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_impact_statement
description: Block: impact statement — oversized centered serif NO LIMITS display headline in lime-off green on the dark-green background, isolated with generous vertical whitespace (reference: segment-06.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_helmet_grid
description: Block: helmet grid — THE HELMET eyebrow over three tall portrait cards masked to helmet silhouettes, each a distinct fictional livery with a numbered 01/02/03 index label in the lower-left (reference: segment-07.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_collaborators_marquee
description: Block: collaborators marquee — COLLABS eyebrow over an edge-faded horizontal marquee row of at least five monochrome fictional partner symbols/wordmarks on the dark-green ground (reference: segment-08.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_social_video_card
description: Block: social video card — SOCIAL eyebrow over a single large rounded video card with a reflective helmet poster image and a lime HOVER TO PLAY pill badge in the lower-right corner (reference: segment-08.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: block_fidelity_footer_statement_newsletter
description: Block: footer statement and newsletter — large split-text serif brand statement DRIVEN BY THE FANS. BUILT FOR THE FUTURE. with the first word filled lime, a second partner-mark marquee, the AVERY VALE wordmark, newsletter signup, and a bottom legal row with copyright (reference: segment-08.png and the tail of overview.png). Score this block against the reference segment: 5 = identical position, layout, spacing, alignment, text, fonts, colors, and imagery; 4 = minor imperfections (misalignment under 2px, one minor text or formatting slip); 3 = partial match (noticeable misalignment or spacing drift, multiple text discrepancies); 2 = poor match (significant misalignment, mostly inconsistent layout); 1 = missing or completely misplaced.

name: specified_motion_states_present
description: Reference-required motion states are present: LOAD VALE preloader clear, menu ellipse expand/collapse (~750ms), pinned horizontal media travel, STORE split reveal, helmet clip-path reveal, and character fills — verified via the real UI/scroll path.

name: responsive_patterns_match_reference
description: Responsive behavior matches the reference patterns at the product breakpoints (992/991/767/479): single-row ~100px nav at ≥992 vs ~140px wrapped mobile nav at 390, three-across helmet cards vs single column (~326×435), menu photo grid hidden at 390.

name: store_and_hamburger_styling_match_spec
description: STORE is a filled lime pill (background rgb(210, 255, 0), dark text, bag icon, radius near 0.74rem/~12px); hamburger and menu close are square outlined buttons about 60×60 with rounded corners — not unstyled browser defaults.

name: display_vs_body_hierarchy
description: Typography hierarchy clearly distinguishes serif display treatment (impact, menu items, footer statement) from Mona Sans Variable body/labels across the homepage.

name: component_states_match_spec
description: Default/hover/disabled states match the spec where specified: HOME current-item lime stroke vs muted green rgb(180, 184, 165), Subscribe disabled until valid email, helmet hover scale/reveal/index lime, video placeholder fade.

name: palette_surfaces_and_accent_exact
description: Color treatments match exactly: dark-green base/menu/footer rgb(40, 44, 32); cream hero rgb(239, 239, 229); off-white text rgb(244, 244, 237); lime accent rgb(210, 255, 0) reserved for STORE, current-menu stroke, and hover highlights — not flooding large section backgrounds.

name: microinteractions_match_spec_timing
description: Hover/press microinteractions follow the reference language and timing: menu overlay 750ms cubic-bezier(0.65, 0.05, 0, 1), STORE split ~0.4s on the same easing, helmet reveal 0.75s, video placeholder opacity 0.3s — verified on the real controls.

name: hardened_surfaces_match_token_system
description: Race calendar, press-kit drawer, and command palette match the dual-tone token system (dark-green rgb(40,44,32), off-white text, lime accents) rather than introducing a second unrelated UI theme.

name: single_page_homepage_title
description: On load the app serves a single homepage at route / (no other route is built), and the browser tab title reads exactly 2025 Apex Grand Prix Driver — Avery Vale

name: preloader_shows_then_clears
description: On a fresh load a full-bleed lime preloader shows the text LOAD VALE and then clears within about one to two seconds to reveal the homepage rather than staying on screen blocking content

name: fixed_nav_contents
description: A fixed nav stays pinned to the top over all sections and shows, left to right, a stacked AVERY VALE text wordmark (AVERY above VALE), a centered original AV monogram, a lime STORE button, a PRESS KIT control, and a square hamburger button

name: menu_overlay_contents
description: After clicking the hamburger, a full-screen dark-green menu overlay covers the viewport listing HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, the social labels CLIPS, PHOTOS, VIDEO, LIVE, and the business contact hello@averyvale.example

name: home_item_current_stroke
description: When the menu is open, the HOME item is marked as the current item with a lime telemetry stroke drawn through its label while ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR are not

name: menu_closes_on_x_or_escape
description: After clicking the X control (or pressing Escape) in the open menu, the overlay closes, is no longer visible, and the homepage is interactive again

name: next_race_widget_contents
description: The hero fills at least the first full viewport height and a NEXT RACE widget sits at its lower left showing the eyebrow NEXT RACE, an original circuit outline graphic, the label ALPINE GP, an original laurel graphic, and the fictional team line NOVA RACING SINCE 2019

name: horizontal_track_scroll_linked
description: After scrolling vertically through the pinned horizontal media section, the media row translates sideways so later photo cards come into view without the page navigating away

name: helmet_card_hover_reveal
description: When hovering a helmet grid card with the real pointer, its base image scales up, a masked reveal image wipes in, and the card index label turns lime

name: video_hover_play_fade
description: When hovering the social video card, the local video plays and the placeholder image fades from opaque to transparent, and leaving the card pauses the video and restores the placeholder

name: footer_split_text_and_marquee
description: After scrolling to the footer, a split-text brand statement fills its characters into the lime-off highlight color and a partner logo marquee scrolls

name: hover_feedback_present
description: When hovering the STORE button and nav or footer text links, a visible hover response (split reveal or color shift toward lime) appears

name: helmet_grid_three_indexed_cards
description: A helmet grid shows at least three tall portrait cards masked to a helmet silhouette, each with a different newly authored fictional livery, separate base and reveal artwork, and a visible index label

name: collaborators_marquee_fictional_marks
description: A collaborators strip shows a horizontal marquee of at least five distinct newly drawn fictional partner symbols or wordmarks; plain typed company names do not count as marks

name: video_card_layered_still
description: A social video card shows an original poster image layered over a muted looping locally served VP9 WebM video whose frames visibly change

name: footer_contents
description: The footer shows a large split-text brand statement, a fictional-partner logo marquee, the AVERY VALE wordmark, a newsletter signup form, and the legal links Privacy Policy, Terms, and Cookies

name: chrome_stays_on_homepage
description: Activating any navigation chrome (wordmark, monogram, menu items, socials, business contact, legal links, Store button) scrolls within the homepage or performs a no-op without reloading the document or leaving the origin

name: hero_composition_with_silhouette
description: The hero shows a newly authored fictional racer portrait/cutout composed in depth with an off-white topographic field, translucent organic blobs, and soft shadow or reflection layers behind the navigation chrome

name: media_strip_six_cards
description: The horizontal media strip holds at least six visually distinct newly authored or generated fictional-racer editorial images, rather than repeated thumbnails or flat color placeholders

name: impact_statement_present
description: An impact statement section between the media strip and the helmet grid renders an oversized display headline in the lime-off color

name: menu_photo_grid_and_texture
description: The open menu overlay shows a newly drawn topographic line pattern and a staggered grid of at least four distinct original fictional editorial photos separate from the horizontal-strip set

name: subscribe_disabled_until_valid
description: The newsletter form has one email field and a Subscribe control; Subscribe stays disabled while email is empty or fails the subscribe field contract (exactly one @, non-empty local part, domain segment containing a dot), and enables only once the value satisfies that contract

name: menu_flow_probe
description: Scripted menu flow: scroll partway down the page and note the scroll position, click the hamburger, confirm the overlay covers the viewport with the HOME item showing its current-item stroke; activate the X control and confirm the overlay closes with scroll position unchanged; reopen the menu and activate ON TRACK, confirm the overlay closes and the page scrolls within / to the horizontal media section without a document reload, and the fixed nav stays pinned

name: newsletter_flow_probe
description: Scripted newsletter flow: type an invalid email (e.g. missing @ or domain without a dot) and confirm Subscribe stays disabled with an inline error naming the email field; correct it to fan@averyvale.example and confirm the error clears and Subscribe enables; submit and confirm an inline confirmation appears, the field empties, and press-kit JSON newsletter equals that email; confirm no navigation or document reload occurred at any step

name: video_hover_cycle_probe
description: Scripted video flow: hover the social video card, confirm playback starts and the placeholder fades; move the pointer off mid-fade and confirm the placeholder returns to fully opaque and the video pauses; repeat the hover-and-leave cycle at least twice more and confirm it keeps working with no stuck intermediate state

name: reload_baseline_probe
description: Scripted reload baseline: open the menu, select a race, shortlist a helmet, open PRESS KIT, submit the newsletter with a valid email, then reload; confirm the preloader plays again, menu/press kit/palette are closed, race selections and shortlist are empty, the newsletter field is empty with no confirmation, and no prior visit state persists

name: rapid_menu_toggle_settles
description: Rapidly clicking the hamburger and the X control in succession never leaves the overlay stuck half-open or the page inert; the menu always settles fully open or fully closed

name: escape_when_closed_noop
description: Pressing Escape while the menu is closed changes nothing on the page

name: empty_submit_blocked
description: Submitting the newsletter form with an empty field is blocked: an inline message names the email field and no confirmation appears

name: double_subscribe_single_confirmation
description: Double-activating the Subscribe control on one valid email shows exactly one confirmation message

name: webgl_fallback_hero_static
description: With WebGL rendering unavailable, the hero shows a static composition of the same artwork and every section, control, and flow remains present and usable

name: menu_items_scroll_in_page
description: With the menu open, activating HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, or CALENDAR scrolls within the same homepage document to the hero, horizontal media strip, social video section, or race calendar respectively, closes the overlay, and does not load another route or origin

name: authored_asset_surface_inventory
description: Across the rendered page, every major reference media surface is populated: the layered hero portrait, six distinct horizontal-strip images, at least four separate menu photos, three two-state helmet designs, five fictional partner marks, the social poster and changing-frame video, and original monogram/circuit/laurel/telemetry/icon artwork are all visible in their intended sections

name: race_calendar_six_seeded_races
description: The race calendar section lists exactly six seeded fictional races in order with circuit, ISO date, and status: Alpine GP 2025-03-16 Upcoming; Bayfront Circuit 2025-04-06 Upcoming; Ridgeway GP 2025-05-04 Completed; Solstice GP 2025-06-15 Upcoming; Meridian Night Run 2025-07-20 Upcoming; Cascade Finale 2025-09-07 Upcoming

name: race_select_updates_count
description: Clicking a race row toggles a visible lime selected treatment and the Selected races N readout updates immediately to match the count of selected rows

name: race_status_filter
description: The race calendar status filter options All, Upcoming, and Completed filter visible rows; selecting Completed shows Ridgeway GP and hides Upcoming rows without navigating away

name: media_and_helmet_shortlist
description: Each horizontal editorial card and each helmet card exposes a Shortlist control; toggling it adds or removes that asset from the media shortlist and updates a visible shortlist count in the chrome

name: press_kit_live_preview_formats
description: Activating PRESS KIT opens a press-kit drawer with JSON, Markdown, and ICS tabs whose live preview includes Avery Vale / Nova Racing, selected race circuits and YYYY-MM-DD dates, shortlisted asset labels/indexes, and newsletter email or the exact marker none

name: press_kit_copy_and_download
description: Inside the press kit, Copy copies the active format preview and shows a brief copied confirmation, and Download offers avery-vale-press-kit.json, .md, or .ics matching the active tab

name: ics_matches_selected_races
description: With three races selected, the ICS preview is a VCALENDAR (VERSION:2.0, PRODID:-//Avery Vale//Press Kit//EN) with exactly three VEVENT blocks each carrying UID equal to that race's uid from the JSON races array, DTSTART;VALUE=DATE as YYYYMMDD, SUMMARY equal to circuit, and STATUS CONFIRMED or CANCELLED matching Upcoming/Completed; with zero selected, it has zero VEVENT entries

name: command_palette_opens_and_navigates
description: Pressing Meta+K or Control+K opens a command palette listing Go HOME, Go ON TRACK, Go OFF TRACK, Go CALENDAR, Open press kit, Undo, and Redo; activating Go CALENDAR closes the palette and scrolls within / to the race calendar

name: undo_redo_shortlist_and_races
description: After shortlisting an asset or selecting a race, Undo reverses that mutation (count and selected treatment restore); Redo reapplies it; the stack covers at least twenty such mutations

name: race_record_field_contract_visible
description: Each of the six seeded race rows shows circuit, a YYYY-MM-DD date, and a status chip that is exactly Upcoming or Completed; Selected races N matches the count of lime-selected rows

name: race_status_editor_enum
description: Opening Edit status on a race and submitting Upcoming or Completed updates that row's chip immediately; submitting a value outside that closed enum shows an inline error naming status and leaves the chip unchanged

name: press_kit_json_field_contract
description: The press-kit JSON preview includes required keys schemaVersion (integer 1), driver Avery Vale, team Nova Racing, season 2025, newsletter (contract-valid email or none), races (selected race records only, each showing id, circuit, date, status, selected true, and uid), shortlist (each entry kind helmet or editorial, non-empty label <= 60 chars, index >= 1), and generatedAt ending in Z

name: press_kit_import_restores_session
description: After clearing selections, Import of a previously exported valid press-kit JSON restores Selected races N, shortlist count, selected treatments, and JSON/ICS previews to match that payload without a reload

name: shortlist_asset_field_contract
description: After shortlisting one helmet and one editorial card, the press-kit JSON shortlist array contains exactly two entries whose kind values are helmet and editorial, each with a non-empty label of at most 60 characters and an index integer >= 1

name: newsletter_subscribe_field_contract
description: A successful Subscribe with fan@averyvale.example records that email as the session subscriber; the press-kit JSON newsletter field equals fan@averyvale.example; an address missing @ or whose domain has no dot keeps Subscribe disabled with an inline error naming email

name: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_resets
description: Starting on the homepage: open the menu, select a race, shortlist a helmet, type a valid email into the newsletter field, and hover the social video card; then reload. All facets coherently reset (menu/press kit/palette closed, selections and shortlist empty, newsletter empty with no confirmation, video not mid-hover-play) — never a mixed partial survival. In-memory genre: nothing persists.

name: subscribe_enablement_tracks_email_validity
description: Derived-view sensitivity: type an invalid email and confirm Subscribe stays disabled with an inline error; correct to a valid email and confirm the error clears and Subscribe enables — the control state changes meaningfully with the input.

name: menu_home_stroke_echo
description: Cross-view echo: open the menu and confirm HOME shows the lime telemetry stroke while ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR do not; close and reopen without reload — HOME remains the current stroked item.

name: newsletter_confirmation_count_delta
description: Count-delta integrity: with a valid email ready, note that zero confirmation messages are visible, submit Subscribe once, and confirm exactly one confirmation message appears with no off-by-one duplicate.

name: invalid_vs_valid_email_outcomes_differ
description: Input-dependent output: attempt the newsletter flow with an invalid email (Subscribe disabled / error, no confirmation) then with a valid email (Subscribe enabled, submit shows confirmation and clears the field); the two outcomes differ as the inputs dictate.

name: interleaved_menu_and_newsletter
description: Interleaved-flow integrity: open the menu, close it via X, type a valid email, open the menu again, close via Escape, then submit the newsletter; the confirmation appears and the menu remains closed — neither flow corrupted the other.

name: video_hover_leave_round_trip
description: Edge-state round-trip: hover the social video card (playback starts, placeholder fades), leave (paused, placeholder fully opaque), then hover again; playback and placeholder opacity track both transitions with no stuck intermediate state.

name: press_kit_derived_sensitivity
description: Derived-view sensitivity: note the empty press-kit JSON preview, select Alpine GP and Bayfront Circuit and shortlist one helmet, reopen PRESS KIT; the JSON/Markdown now name those two circuits and the shortlisted label, and ICS has exactly two VEVENT entries — not an identical redraw.

name: undo_round_trip_race_select
description: Edge-state round-trip: select Solstice GP (Selected races increments), Undo (selection clears and count decrements), Redo (selection and count restore).

name: newsletter_echoes_into_press_kit
description: Cross-view echo: submit newsletter with fan@averyvale.example, open PRESS KIT JSON; the preview includes that subscriber email; a different valid email on a fresh flow yields a different email in the kit.

name: press_kit_import_round_trip_probe
description: Export then import round-trip probe: select Alpine GP and Bayfront Circuit and shortlist one helmet; copy JSON; Undo both selections and the shortlist; Import the JSON; Selected races is 2, shortlist count is 1, ICS has two VEVENT entries, and JSON races arrays name both circuits.

name: status_enum_echoes_into_ics
description: Cross-view echo: with Ridgeway GP selected, Edit status to Upcoming; ICS STATUS for that VEVENT becomes CONFIRMED; set Completed and it becomes CANCELLED — not a stale STATUS.

name: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable
description: Every interactive control (STORE, PRESS KIT, hamburger, menu items, socials, legal links, shortlist controls, race rows, status filter, Undo, Redo, email field, Subscribe, palette search/results) is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space).

name: menu_modal_focus_trap_and_return
description: The menu overlay behaves as a modal dialog: it traps focus while open, closes on Escape, and returns focus to the hamburger button on close.

name: media_have_accessible_names
description: Editorial images, helmet cards, partner marks, and the social video poster carry descriptive alt text or accessible labels where they convey content.

name: newsletter_feedback_uses_live_region
description: Newsletter inline error and confirmation messages are announced through an aria-live region as well as shown visually.

name: newsletter_field_has_explicit_label
description: The newsletter email field uses an explicit label association (or equivalent accessible name).

name: headings_logical_order
description: Section headings follow a logical heading order through the homepage with no skipped levels that break the outline.

name: landmarks_nav_main_footer
description: The document exposes HTML-first landmarks: header/nav chrome, a main content landmark wrapping the homepage sections, and a footer landmark.

name: contrast_cream_and_dark_sections
description: Text over the cream hero and dark-green sections, and all control labels, meet WCAG AA contrast; lime STORE button text on lime stays high-contrast (dark text on rgb(210, 255, 0)).

name: semantic_interactive_chrome
description: Interactive chrome uses real controls (button, label, native links) with semantic roles; custom overlays expose matching ARIA roles only where native elements cannot carry the behavior.

name: reduced_motion_settles_immediately
description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, scroll-linked animation settles immediately, the LOAD VALE preloader does not block content, in-page jumps use instant scroll, and continuous marquees do not run — while the page remains fully scrollable and operable.

name: split_text_aria_label_on_container
description: Split-text headlines (impact statement and footer brand statement) keep the original phrase as an aria-label on the heading container while individual character/word spans are aria-hidden from the accessibility tree.

name: hero_webgl_labelled_or_decorative
description: The hero canvas or WebGL layer is treated as decorative or labelled: role=img with a descriptive aria-label, or aria-hidden when a static silhouette sibling already conveys the artwork.

name: press_kit_and_palette_modal_a11y
description: The press-kit drawer and command palette each behave as modal dialogs: Escape closes them, focus is trapped while open, and focus returns to the control that opened them.

name: shortlist_controls_accessible_names
description: Icon-only shortlist controls on helmet and editorial cards expose descriptive accessible names.

name: selection_counts_live_region
description: Selected races N and shortlist count changes are announced via an aria-live region as well as shown visually.
